routine: Woke up early, walked to school, came home for lunch, played outside until dark
Weekends spent reading comics or exploring the nearby fields
facilities: Stable home with both parents present
Access to a small personal book collection
scarcity: No internet or computer at home
Limited exposure to the world outside the town

environment.locality: Quiet residential area in a small town

guardianRelation: Father was strict about academics but distant emotionally
Mother was warm and protective, handled all household decisions
siblingRelation: One older sister who was high-achieving and often held up as the example
friendsRelation: Small group of neighborhood kids, mostly surface-level friendships
One close friend who shared interest in reading and imagining stories
mentorRelation: A 4th grade teacher who noticed curiosity and encouraged independent reading

elements: Middle-class families, government employees and small business owners
Conservative social norms, emphasis on respect for elders
societalValues: Education as the primary path to success
Community reputation matters more than individual expression

moments.situation: Won a district-level drawing competition unexpectedly
moments.conclusion: Felt a strange mix of pride and discomfort with the attention — preferred the act of drawing over being recognized for it

moments.situation: Father publicly compared academic performance to older sister at a family gathering
moments.conclusion: Stopped sharing report cards voluntarily and began keeping achievements private

moments.situation: Best friend moved to another city at the end of 6th grade
moments.conclusion: Did not make an effort to replace the friendship — retreated into books and solitary activities instead

illness: Mild anxiety that started in second year, never formally diagnosed

routine: Classes in the morning, self-study or projects in the afternoon, late-night reading or coding
Skipped social events frequently to work on personal projects
facilities: Fast internet for the first time
Access to a large university library
Freedom to structure own time without parental oversight
scarcity: Tight monthly budget from parents, no luxury spending
No emotional support system — parents were far away and communication was infrequent

guardianRelation: Minimal contact — monthly phone calls about grades and finances only
siblingRelation: Sister was abroad for her own studies, relationship became cordial but distant
friendsRelation: A group of 4-5 close friends from the hostel who stayed up late debating ideas
One friend who constantly pushed boundaries and introduced new music, films, philosophy
mentorRelation: A computer science professor who gave hard feedback but genuinely cared about intellectual growth

elements: Students from across the country with diverse backgrounds
Competitive academic culture mixed with creative underground scene
societalValues: Performance and placement rankings defined social status
Intellectual curiosity was respected but only if it led to measurable output

moments.situation: Built a side project that got featured on a tech blog during third year
moments.conclusion: Felt validated but immediately felt pressure to repeat the success — started three new projects and finished none

moments.situation: Had a falling out with the closest hostel friend over a misunderstanding that was never resolved
moments.conclusion: Realized the pattern of withdrawing instead of confronting — let the friendship die rather than having an uncomfortable conversation

moments.situation: Failed a subject for the first time in final year due to skipping classes for a personal project
moments.conclusion: Parents were upset but the experience taught that external structure was something actively resisted, not just neglected

moments.situation: Spent an entire semester mostly alone after the friend group dispersed for internships
moments.conclusion: Discovered that solitude was not just comfortable but actively preferred — productivity and clarity peaked during this period
